nodes.label: LIMINAL (project)
nodes.rationale: First-person Backrooms horror (single-player + WebSocket co-op): wake in an infinite Level-0 office floor, collect 5 snus tins to unlock the single real exit while hunted by shadowy vultos that must never be seen up close outside a jumpscare.

nodes.label: Vision Pillars
nodes.rationale: Six inviolable design pillars that constrain every system: dread over gore, sick CRT signal, sound-carried gameplay, watchers not monsters, static maze, no HUD.

nodes.label: Dread Over Gore
nodes.rationale: No blood, no monster close-ups, no death screens except sanctioned endings — the player's imagination does the work.

nodes.label: The Signal Is Sick
nodes.rationale: Everything is watched through an old-TV/CRT filter (scanlines, snow, tracking tears); more dread means a sicker signal.

nodes.label: Sound Carries the Game
nodes.rationale: The fluorescent hum bed never stops (only allowed silences: ~2.4s post-jumpscare and after a chase vanishes); distance and danger are communicated by audio, not UI.

nodes.label: Watchers, Not Monsters
nodes.rationale: Vultos observe from afar, recede when looked at directly, vanish inside 6m if approached; chases are the exception (1-2 per run, escapable by cornering).

nodes.label: Static Maze Invariant
nodes.rationale: Maze layout is a pure hash-based function of cell coordinates — required for co-op sync; per-player mutation must never be reintroduced.

nodes.label: No HUD
nodes.rationale: No map, no flashlight, no objective markers; the only exception is a transient snus counter that fades in for seconds.

nodes.label: NetManager Autoload
nodes.rationale: Custom WebSocket relay multiplayer (scripts/autoloads/net_manager.gd); position broadcast at 20 Hz.

nodes.label: AudioManager Autoload
nodes.rationale: Pooled SFX + music, web autoplay-gated; the mandated route for all audio (no ad-hoc AudioStreamPlayers except positional ones on short-lived nodes).

nodes.label: GameManager Autoload
nodes.rationale: Run clock (run_time), look-back counter, restart handling.

nodes.label: Game World Glue (game_world.gd)
nodes.rationale: World glue: environment, ambient bed, audio ducking, endings, co-op wiring.

nodes.label: Maze Manager (maze_manager.gd)
nodes.rationale: Streaming static grid: open-plan halls, pillars, sparse wall slabs, spaced fluorescent panels; corridor_path() BFS used by the entity; exit at fixed cell (14,-16) unlocked by snus.

nodes.label: Entity Director (entity_director.gd)
nodes.rationale: The vultos: PEEK/JUMP/CHASE/STALK state machine paced off GameManager.run_time; chases follow corridor waypoints (never phase through walls); 2s without line of sight = instant vanish.

nodes.label: Snus Manager (snus_manager.gd)
nodes.rationale: 5 tins at fixed cells; co-op-shared pickup.

nodes.label: CRT Overlay (overlay.gd)
nodes.rationale: CanvasLayer with the CRT post shader, fade rect, ending text; drives the shader's dread/pulse uniforms.

nodes.label: Tuning Knobs File (tuning.gd)
nodes.rationale: ALL horror pacing/difficulty knobs live here; game feel is changed by editing this file only.

nodes.label: gl_compatibility Renderer Constraint
nodes.rationale: WebGL 2 web export mandates the Compatibility renderer: no Forward+-only features (volumetric fog, SDFGI); depth fog + glow are fine (4.3+).

nodes.label: QA Headless Runner Verification
nodes.rationale: No local Godot binary; QA runs happen through the platform's headless runner (Godot 4.6.1) writing .qa/<timestamp>/report.json + console.log — ask the user to run QA rather than claiming runtime verification.

nodes.label: Co-op Relay Mode (2-4 players)
nodes.rationale: Public internet relay (no port forwarding); host picks group size, gets a room code; identical maze for all, shared snus; one player reaching exit saves the team; downed players spectate; scares are personal per player; LIMINAL_RELAY env var overrides relay for local dev.

nodes.label: Tesana Export Guide
nodes.rationale: Explains the zip exported from Tesana (2026-07-18, game-20260718-492248f9): what .gd/.tscn/.tres/.import/.uid files are, why .godot/ cache is excluded, headless re-export commands, macOS Gatekeeper workaround.

nodes.label: Backrooms Entity SFX Vol. 1 (juanjo_sound) — Terms
nodes.author: juanjo_sound
nodes.rationale: License: usable in commercial and non-commercial projects, modifiable; cannot be resold/redistributed as standalone files or claimed as own creation; attribution optional but appreciated.

nodes.label: _post_import (character orientation fix)
nodes.rationale: Tesana auto-generated EditorScenePostImport: applies +PI Y rotation at IMPORT time so baked character GLBs face -Z (Vector3.MODEL_FRONT, what look_at() and atan2(-dx,-dz) expect) — game code never needs a magic flip.

nodes.label: TesanaWorldEditor Autoload (scene_export.gd)
nodes.rationale: Auto-generated live scene-tree broadcaster for the Tesana Studio World tab (WS 127.0.0.1:5180, HTTP asset server :5181). Production safety: _ready() bails in exported template builds unless TESANA_EMBEDDED=1, and in TESANA_QA_TEST runs; binds loopback only. Runs PROCESS_MODE_ALWAYS so it keeps polling while the tree is paused for editing.

nodes.label: _maybe_broadcast_snapshot
nodes.rationale: SHA-256 hash dedup skips unchanged snapshots; per-peer outbound-buffer throttle skips a round if the previous snapshot is still queued (browser slow to consume 2-3 MiB JSON) to avoid ERR_OUT_OF_MEMORY even at 16-32 MB buffers.

nodes.label: _infer_glb_anchors
nodes.rationale: Procedurally-built characters lack scene_file_path, so the viewer would show wireframes; walks post-order and tags the deepest named Node3D ancestor of GLB-referencing meshes with inferred_scene_file_path.

nodes.label: _dispatch_op (edit op router)
nodes.rationale: Routes set_position/set_rotation/set_scale/set_property/delete_node/clone_node ops; batch ops apply in one frame so visual and collision move together.

nodes.label: _handle_http_request (static asset server)
nodes.rationale: Minimal HTTP/1.1 in GDScript serving res:// files with CORS for the Vite viewer; one request per connection, whole file in memory, path-traversal guarded.

nodes.label: CRT Post Filter fragment()
nodes.rationale: Fullscreen old-TV filter: barrel curvature, chromatic aberration, scanlines + interlace, TV snow, VHS tracking tears, rolling band, vignette. Compatibility (WebGL 2) pipeline; budget 3 screen samples/px, no loops. Grade target: dark A24 still — red/cyan fringing, drained sepia.

nodes.label: Player Fantasy
nodes.rationale: "I am alone in a place that shouldn't exist, and something is watching me... The TV signal I'm seeing this through is as sick as I am scared." — the emotional contract every system serves.

nodes.label: Player Journey (target pacing)
nodes.rationale: 0-1min calm; 1-5min first distant watcher + unexplained sounds; 5-8min jumpscares armed (max 3/run, >=3min apart, ~25s false-security calm before each); 8-10min chases armed (max 2/run, cornering escape); 10+min permanent slow stalker, standing still >6s is death; exit at cell (14,-16) after 5 snus.

nodes.label: Tuning Knobs Table
nodes.rationale: Every knob in scripts/tuning.gd with its intent contract, e.g. JUMP_ARM_TIME=300 (no jumpscares in the first 5 minutes ever), PEEK_VANISH_DIST=6 (a vulto is never seen up close outside a jumpscare), CHASE_SPEED=2.95 vs player 2.4 (escape barely possible), LOS_LOSE_TIME=2.0 (cornering is the escape mechanic). Retune freely within intent.

nodes.label: Grade & Ambience References
nodes.rationale: Visual: dark A24-still grade — crushed brown-black shadows, isolated warm panels with bloom, heavy vignette, red/cyan aberration, sepia drain. Ambience: A24 Backrooms room-tone drone (menu theme at 0.72x pitch, DRONE_VOL -16 dB) + dark mains hum (0.92x) + distant HVAC.

nodes.label: Acceptance Criteria
nodes.rationale: Checklist enforcing the pillars: genuinely dark zones always exist; never absolute silence outside sanctioned beats; impossible to see a vulto up close outside a jumpscare; 2s broken LOS vanishes the chaser with a hard sound cut; false-security calm precedes every jumpscare; no HUD/map/flashlight/gore; maze identical across clients.

nodes.label: Endings (Exit / Caught / Secret)
nodes.rationale: Exit: reach the unlocked exit, whole team escapes in co-op. Caught: chase touch or stalk kill — black screen, dead silence, restart (co-op: personal down + spectate). Secret: stand still 60s inside an anomaly room ('Now you are the one waiting at the corner.').

nodes.label: LLM Knowledge Gap Warning
nodes.rationale: LLM training covers Godot up to ~4.3 (cutoff May 2025) but the runner is Godot 4.6 (Jan 2026); 4.4/4.5/4.6 changes must always be cross-referenced from this directory before suggesting API calls.

nodes.label: Jolt Physics Default (4.6)
nodes.rationale: Jolt is the default 3D physics engine for new projects since 4.6 (better determinism/stability); existing projects keep their setting; some HingeJoint3D properties (damp) only work with GodotPhysics.

nodes.label: Glow Before Tonemapping (4.6)
nodes.rationale: Glow now processes before tonemapping (was after) with screen blending — existing glow setups look different and need intensity/blend adjustment in WorldEnvironment.

nodes.label: Shader Baker (4.5)
nodes.rationale: Pre-compiles shaders — reportedly 20x faster startup on some demos.

nodes.label: IK System Restored via SkeletonModifier3D (4.6)
nodes.rationale: CCDIK, FABRIK, Jacobian IK, Spline IK, TwoBoneIK reintroduced as SkeletonModifier3D nodes.

nodes.label: Godot Deprecated APIs Replacement Table
nodes.rationale: If an agent suggests any API in the Deprecated column it MUST be replaced with the Use Instead column (yield->await, instance()->instantiate(), TileMap->TileMapLayer, string connect->typed signals, etc.).

nodes.label: ripgrep Has No gdscript Type (use glob *.gd)
nodes.rationale: *.gd is registered under ripgrep's gap type (GAP language); rg --type gdscript is a hard error and never executes — always filter with --glob "*.gd" (or glob: "*.gd" in the Grep tool).

nodes.label: Godot Audio Module Reference
nodes.rationale: No audio API breaks in 4.4-4.6; canonical patterns: bus volume control, 3D spatial audio setup, and object pooling of AudioStreamPlayers instead of runtime creation.

nodes.label: Godot Input Module Reference
nodes.rationale: 4.6 dual-focus: mouse/touch focus is separate from keyboard/gamepad focus; grab_focus() only affects keyboard/gamepad focus.

nodes.label: Godot Specialist Agent
nodes.rationale: Authority on Godot patterns/APIs; guides GDScript vs C# vs GDExtension decisions, node/scene architecture, signals, resources; delegates to gdscript/shader/gdextension sub-specialists; must not make game design decisions or add dependencies without technical-director sign-off.

nodes.label: Godot GDScript Specialist Agent
nodes.rationale: Owns GDScript code quality: mandatory static typing, naming conventions, signal architecture (signals up, method calls down), coroutine rules, autoload/resource/composition patterns, performance rules, anti-pattern review.

nodes.label: Godot Shader Specialist Agent
nodes.rationale: Owns .gdshader authoring, renderer selection (Forward+/Mobile/Compatibility), particle shaders, post-processing, GPU budgets (16.6ms frame allocation), and shader anti-patterns (no branching, minimal texture samples).

nodes.label: Agent Collaboration Protocol
nodes.rationale: Shared workflow in all specialist agents: read the design doc, ask architecture questions, propose architecture with trade-offs before implementing, stop on spec ambiguity, get explicit approval before writing files, offer next steps. Agents are collaborative implementers, not autonomous code generators.

nodes.label: LIMINAL GDScript Rules
nodes.rationale: Path-scoped rules for scripts/**: tuning knobs only in tuning.gd; static typing; delta time for motion; signals for cross-system communication (world never reaches into UI); guard asset loads with ResourceLoader.exists(); audio through AudioManager; entity state machines log transitions; static maze invariant; check deprecated-apis.md for post-4.3 APIs.

nodes.label: LIMINAL Shader Rules
nodes.rationale: Path-scoped rules for assets/shaders/**: [type]_[purpose].gdshader naming, never inline shader strings; header comment with purpose/uniform drivers/pipeline/budget; no magic numbers; Compatibility pipeline only (no compute, no stencil); no dynamic branching or texture reads in loops; CRT filter screen-sample budget is 3.
